profile.name: Omer Karaduman
profile.title: Generalist software engineer, automation builder, and coaching-minded problem solver
profile.tagline: A capability atlas: experiences mapped to the patterns they reveal.
profile.summary: This sample map shows how projects, talks, coaching, hobbies, and odd constraints can reveal transferable capabilities. Replace the sample data with your own exported Mosaic JSON.

capabilities.label: Systems thinking
capabilities.description: Seeing relationships, feedback loops, constraints, and unintended consequences.

capabilities.label: Bridge building
capabilities.description: Connecting existing tools, people, and processes instead of replacing everything.

capabilities.label: McGyvering
capabilities.description: Finding practical paths through awkward constraints with available materials.

capabilities.label: Automation
capabilities.description: Turning repeated manual work into reliable, inspectable workflows.

capabilities.label: Data observability
capabilities.description: Making status, trends, and weak signals visible through data.

capabilities.label: AI tooling
capabilities.description: Using AI as a practical amplifier for requirements, analysis, and workflows.

capabilities.label: UX thinking
capabilities.description: Designing tools around adoption, clarity, and user behaviour.

capabilities.label: Coaching
capabilities.description: Helping people surface their own understanding and next move.

capabilities.label: Stakeholder translation
capabilities.description: Translating between technical, operational, and human concerns.

capabilities.label: Public speaking
capabilities.description: Turning ideas into memorable stories for a group.

capabilities.label: Creative expression
capabilities.description: Using music, storytelling, visuals, and play as thinking tools.

capabilities.label: Learning agility
capabilities.description: Getting useful quickly in unfamiliar domains.

principles.label: Bridge, don't replace
principles.description: Adoption often improves when new solutions connect to existing workflows.

principles.label: Constraints fuel creativity
principles.description: Limitations can make the solution sharper rather than weaker.

principles.label: Show the system
principles.description: People make better decisions when hidden state becomes visible.

principles.label: Think with your hands
principles.description: Building small artifacts is often the fastest way to discover the real problem.

experiences.title: CopierciN
experiences.summary: A utility born from the need to bridge copied content into workflows where official integration paths were limited.
experiences.rawNarrative: Sample narrative: I wanted a smoother copy/paste workflow, but the ecosystem did not provide the clean integration points I wanted. Instead of waiting for a perfect API, I explored what was already available and built a practical bridge.
experiences.publicNarrative: CopierciN is a small example of a recurring pattern: when a direct integration route is blocked, look for the behaviour people already rely on and build a bridge around it.
experiences.challenge: There was no obvious public SDK path for the workflow I wanted to support.
experiences.approach: Reverse-engineered available artifacts and used lightweight integration tricks to make the workflow usable.
experiences.impact: Turned a blocked workflow into a working tool and shaped a long-running preference for practical bridges over total redesigns.
revealedPatterns: I prefer bridging existing workflows over replacing them.
I enjoy solving useful problems under awkward constraints.

experiences.title: Defect observability dashboard
experiences.summary: A reporting workflow that made defect state, age, ownership, and trend information easier to inspect.
experiences.rawNarrative: Sample narrative: The available exports showed current status, but historical trend was not naturally available. The interesting part was not only building a dashboard, but creating a repeatable data trail so the system could start telling a story over time.
experiences.publicNarrative: The dashboard mattered less as a report and more as an observability layer: a way to turn static status checking into shared trend-aware conversations.
experiences.challenge: Important status information existed, but history and trend context were hard to see.
experiences.approach: Combined recurring snapshots, transformation logic, and dashboard views into a more observable workflow.
experiences.impact: Helped move from static status checking to trend-aware conversations.
revealedPatterns: I turn vague operational pain into inspectable workflows.
I look for ways to make hidden system state visible enough for better conversations.

experiences.title: AI Day facilitation and support
experiences.summary: Supporting an AI-focused day by translating possibilities into usable conversations and next steps.
experiences.rawNarrative: Sample narrative: AI conversations easily become either hype or fear. The useful layer is helping people connect the technology to their own work without losing the human and organizational context.
experiences.publicNarrative: This experience sits at the intersection of AI tooling, facilitation, and coaching: helping people move from abstract possibility to grounded next steps.
experiences.challenge: AI adoption discussions can stay too abstract or tool-focused.
experiences.approach: Used facilitation, examples, and translation between technical and non-technical viewpoints.
experiences.impact: Created room for more practical and grounded AI conversations.
revealedPatterns: I translate emerging technology into human, organizational, and practical terms.
I am drawn to adoption questions, not only tool capability questions.

experiences.title: Clarinet, stage practice, and learning in public
experiences.summary: A creative learning path that combines deliberate practice, performance pressure, feedback, and identity.
experiences.rawNarrative: Sample narrative: Music is not a software project, but it exposes many of the same patterns: practice loops, constraints, performance anxiety, feedback, collaboration, and the strange gap between knowing and doing.
experiences.publicNarrative: Clarinet practice reveals a non-software learning pattern: making progress while visible, imperfect, and dependent on feedback.
experiences.challenge: Performing and learning an expressive instrument requires comfort with imperfection and feedback.
experiences.approach: Combined practice, mentorship, equipment experiments, and small performance contexts.
experiences.impact: Strengthened learning agility, creative expression, and tolerance for visible iteration.
revealedPatterns: I learn by creating feedback loops between practice, reflection, and public exposure.
Creative practice gives me another language for understanding systems, people, and growth.

experiences.title: Mosaic capability atlas
experiences.summary: A self-made portfolio alternative that maps experiences to capabilities, principles, and time.
experiences.rawNarrative: Sample narrative: A traditional portfolio would show projects. I wanted something that shows the patterns behind the projects: what each experience strengthened, what it revealed, and how seemingly unrelated domains connect.
experiences.publicNarrative: Mosaic is both the artifact and the argument: generalist value becomes easier to see when experiences are mapped to capabilities, principles, and revealed patterns.
experiences.challenge: Generalist value is difficult to communicate through a linear CV or project list.
experiences.approach: Designed a static, data-driven map where experiences can be explored through capabilities, principles, and timeline.
experiences.impact: Creates a more memorable way to introduce the kind of engineer and coach someone is becoming.
revealedPatterns: I build artifacts to understand and communicate fuzzy identity-level problems.
I connect software, coaching, storytelling, and visual systems when a plain document undersells the idea.
